schema_notes: Each framework key maps to an array of version objects. Add new versions as additional array entries; select_framework_versions.py resolves the correct version for a given as-of date. v1.2 adds optional iam_control_family_refs at the framework-level — clauses indexed by the iam_control_families taxonomy below. The security-auditor agent reads this block directly (cat the JSON or query with python json.load) when scoping IAM/CIAM/RBAC/workload-identity controls per framework, so clause anchors stay co-located with version data instead of drifting in agent prose.

iam_control_families.authentication: Verifying the asserted identity of a user, device, service, or workload (proof-of-possession, multi-factor, FIDO2/WebAuthn, mTLS, OIDC ID-token verification).
iam_control_families.authorization: Determining what a verified identity is permitted to do (RBAC, ABAC, ReBAC, OAuth scopes, custom role grammars, policy engines such as OPA, HCL-defined roles).
iam_control_families.identifier_management: Issuing, rotating, retiring identifiers for humans and non-humans (user IDs, service-principal IDs, SPIFFE IDs, machine identifiers).
iam_control_families.credential_lifecycle: Lifecycle of credentials and tokens (issuance, rotation, expiry, revocation, ephemerality, audience binding, key management).
iam_control_families.session_management: Session establishment, revalidation, idle/absolute timeout, binding to device/IP/risk-signal, logout/single-logout flows.
iam_control_families.joiner_mover_leaver: Provisioning/deprovisioning of access on hire, role change, and termination (SCIM, IGA platforms, automated deprovisioning).
iam_control_families.access_review: Periodic recertification of access, segregation-of-duties analysis, orphan-account detection, privileged-access attestation.
iam_control_families.machine_identity: Identity for non-human principals (services, workloads, agents) with workload-identity federation, ephemeral credentials, and audience-bound tokens — the modern alternative to static secrets.
iam_control_families.federation: Cross-domain identity assertion (SAML 2.0, OIDC federation, SCIM provisioning, B2B partner identity, OIDC-based workload-identity federation between cloud accounts/regions).
iam_control_families.audit_and_monitoring: Identity-event logging, anomaly detection, SIEM/IDP integration, audit-trail integrity for all of the above.

authentication: Art. 32(1)(b) Confidentiality, integrity, availability — appropriate technical measures including authentication
authorization: Art. 25 Data protection by design and by default — least-privilege defaults
identifier_management: Art. 5(1)(c) Data minimisation — applies to identifier scope
credential_lifecycle: Art. 32(1)(a) Pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data
joiner_mover_leaver: Art. 32(4) Persons authorised to process personal data — implies controlled access lifecycle
access_review: Art. 32(1)(d) Process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating effectiveness
machine_identity: Art. 32(1)(b) — applies to systems and services processing personal data, not only humans
federation: Art. 28 Processor obligations — including controller-processor identity boundaries
Art. 32(2) Risks from accidental or unlawful access
audit_and_monitoring: Art. 33 Notification of personal data breach

access_review: §164.308(a)(4)(ii)(C) Access establishment and modification — periodic review
machine_identity: §164.312(a)(2)(i) Unique user identification (interpreted to apply to system identities)
federation: §164.314 Organizational requirements — Business Associate Agreements covering federated/third-party access

CCPA_CPRA.version: CCPA as amended by CPRA regulations

iam_control_family_refs._note: CCPA/CPRA does not enumerate identity-control families; Cal. Civ. Code §1798.81.5 Reasonable security is the umbrella anchor for authn/authz/identifier/credential/session/JML/access-review/machine-identity/audit obligations. Specific clauses below cover where the statute is more explicit.
authentication: Cal. Civ. Code §1798.81.5 Reasonable security — interpreted to require authentication of access to consumer data
authorization: §1798.100(d) Limit collection/use/retention to what is reasonably necessary — least privilege over consumer data
identifier_management: Cal. Civ. Code §1798.81.5 Reasonable security — applies to identifier scope of consumer-data systems
credential_lifecycle: Cal. Civ. Code §1798.81.5 Reasonable security — applies to credential management for consumer-data systems
session_management: Cal. Civ. Code §1798.81.5 Reasonable security — applies to session controls
joiner_mover_leaver: Cal. Civ. Code §1798.81.5 Reasonable security — applies to access lifecycle
access_review: Cal. Civ. Code §1798.81.5 Reasonable security — applies to periodic review
machine_identity: Cal. Civ. Code §1798.81.5 Reasonable security — applies to non-human principals accessing consumer data
federation: §1798.140(ag) Service provider definition
§1798.100(d) Contracted access to personal information

authentication: Art. 9(4)(c) Strong authentication mechanisms and protective measures of cryptographic keys
authorization: Art. 9(4)(c)-(d) Logical access (principle of least privilege, need-to-know) and strong authentication
identifier_management: Art. 9(4)(c) Logical access controls covering identity lifecycle
credential_lifecycle: Art. 9(4)(c) Strong authentication and protective measures of cryptographic keys
Art. 9(4)(d) Sound and comprehensive ICT operations security
session_management: Art. 9(4)(c)-(d) Logical access controls including session protections
joiner_mover_leaver: Art. 9(4)(c) Logical access controls — joiner/mover/leaver implied by least-privilege requirement
Art. 16 Simplified ICT risk management framework — access governance
access_review: Art. 6(8) ICT risk management — periodic review
Art. 9(4)(c) Logical access controls — periodic recertification
machine_identity: Art. 28 ICT third-party risk — programmatic/system access from third parties
federation: Art. 28 ICT third-party arrangements — general principles
Art. 30 Key contractual provisions
Art. 31 Critical ICT third-party providers
audit_and_monitoring: Art. 17-23 ICT-related incident management, classification, and reporting
Art. 9(4)(d) Sound and comprehensive ICT operations security — monitoring/logging

EU_AI_ACT.notes: Phased application dates apply by obligation category.

authentication: Art. 15 Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity — including authentication for high-risk AI system access
authorization: Art. 14 Human oversight — authorisation boundaries for human-in-the-loop

credential_lifecycle: Art. 15 Cybersecurity — credential governance as part of robustness
session_management: Art. 14 Human oversight — session-level intervention capability
joiner_mover_leaver: Art. 14 Human oversight — qualified-personnel access lifecycle

federation: Art. 25 Obligations along the AI value chain — provider/deployer/importer identity boundaries
audit_and_monitoring: Art. 26 Obligations of deployers — including monitoring
